title: SSL Certificate Inventory
content:
# SSL Certificate Inventory (Updated 2026-03-15)

| Domain | Cert Type | Issuer | Expiry Date | Responsible | Deployment |
|------|---------|---------|---------|--------|----------|
| api.example.com | Single-domain OV | DigiCert | 2026-03-31 | Li (Ops Engineer) | nginx-lb-01/02 |
| www.example.com | Single-domain OV | DigiCert | 2026-09-15 | Li (Ops Engineer) | nginx-lb-01/02 |
| pay.example.com | Single-domain EV | GlobalSign | 2026-03-27 | Wang (Ops Engineer) | nginx-pay-01 |
| *.internal.com | Wildcard DV | Let's Encrypt | 2026-04-04 | Zhang (Ops Admin) | gateway-01 |
| m.example.com | Single-domain DV | Let's Encrypt | 2026-07-20 | Zhang (Ops Admin) | nginx-mobile-01 |

## Renewal Reminder Rules
- EV certificates: Begin preparation 60 days before expiry
- OV certificates: Begin renewal 30 days before expiry
- DV certificates: Auto-renew 14 days before expiry

## Notes
- EV certificate renewal requires enterprise verification, 2-5 business day lead time
- Let's Encrypt certificates auto-renew via certbot
- After certificate update, synchronize across all load balancer nodes
author: Security Team

title: SSL Certificate Renewal Process
content:
# SSL Certificate Renewal Process

## DigiCert Certificate Renewal
1. Log in to DigiCert management console
2. Find the certificate and click Renew
3. Generate new CSR: openssl req -new -newkey rsa:2048 -nodes -keyout domain.key -out domain.csr
4. Submit CSR and complete domain validation
5. Download new certificate and deploy

## GlobalSign EV Certificate Renewal
1. Contact GlobalSign account manager 60 days in advance
2. Submit enterprise documents (business license, organization code certificate)
3. Complete phone verification
4. Wait for issuance (typically 2-5 business days)
5. Express lane: Contact account manager to request expedited processing, can be shortened to 1 business day

## Let's Encrypt Certificate Renewal
1. Check certbot service: systemctl status certbot.timer
2. Manual renewal: certbot renew
3. Verify renewal: certbot certificates
4. Reload nginx: nginx -s reload

## Deployment Steps
1. Back up old certificate
2. Upload new certificate to /etc/nginx/ssl/
3. Update nginx config to point to new certificate
4. Test config: nginx -t
5. Reload config: nginx -s reload
6. Verify: openssl s_client -connect domain:443
author: Security Team
